name: Sonendo, Inc.
business: Sonendo is a commercial-stage medical technology company focused on saving teeth from tooth decay, and has developed the GentleWave System, an innovative technology platform designed to treat tooth decay by cleaning and disinfecting the microscopic spaces within teeth without the need to remove tooth structure. The GentleWave System is a Class II device and has received 510(k) clearance from the FDA for preparing, cleaning, and irrigating teeth indicated for root canal therapy (RCT). The company generates revenue primarily from sales of the GentleWave Console and accompanying single-use procedure instruments (PIs) to dental practitioners, including endodontists and general dentists performing a high volume of root canals.

exposures.sentence: We have developed and manufacture the GentleWave System, an innovative technology platform designed to treat tooth decay by cleaning and disinfecting the microscopic spaces within teeth without the need to remove tooth structure.

catalysts.event: ADA code update effective January 1, 2025, may improve insurance reimbursement for GentleWave procedure
catalysts.sentence: Effective January 1, 2025, the American Dental Association (ADA) updated ADA Code 2940, a widely used procedure code previously associated with 'protective restoration,' to clarify that it should also be used to create an 'endodontic seal.'

catalysts.event: Fourth Amendment to Credit Agreement on February 28, 2025, deferring amortization payments and waiving certain covenants subject to financing conditions
catalysts.sentence: On February 28, 2025, we entered into the Fourth Amendment to the Credit Agreement.

catalysts.event: Reverse stock split effected on October 18, 2024 at a 1-for-200 ratio
catalysts.sentence: On October 16, 2024, we filed an amendment to our Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation to implement a 1-for-200 reverse stock split of its issued and outstanding common stock, which was effected on October 18, 2024.

catalysts.event: Company voluntarily suspended SEC reporting obligations on February 27, 2025
catalysts.sentence: On February 27, 2025, we filed a Form 15 certifying the deregistration of our common stock under Section 15(d) of the Exchange Act and suspension of our duty to file reports under Sections 13 and 15(d) of the Exchange Act.

tamClaims.claim: Total annual addressable market in the United States and Canada is approximately $1.9 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Given the average selling price of our products and our estimates on replacement cycle, and the number of root canals performed annually, we estimate that our total annual addressable market in the United States and Canada is approximately $1.9 billion.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 17 million root canal procedures are performed annually in the United States and Canada, accounting for approximately $17 billion in healthcare-related expenditures.
tamClaims.sentence: Our commercial efforts are primarily focused on driving awareness and adoption of our system in our initial target markets of the United States and Canada, where we estimate that approximately 17 million root canal procedures are performed annually, accounting for approximately $17 billion in healthcare-related expenditures.

tamClaims.claim: More than 50 million root canal procedures performed annually on a global basis including the United States and Canada.
tamClaims.sentence: We also believe there is a significant opportunity for our GentleWave System in RCT outside the United States and Canada, with more than 50 million root canal procedures performed annually on a global basis including the United States and Canada.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 175 million procedures for earlier-stage tooth decay performed in the United States each year.
tamClaims.sentence: In addition, we are exploring opportunities to leverage our technology platform beyond RCT to treat cavities in earlier-stage tooth decay, for which we estimate there are approximately 175 million procedures performed in the United States each year.
